content.text:
<timestamp>Thursday, Jul 9, 2026, 3:45 AM (UTC+4)</timestamp>
<user_query>
there is a simple bug in application data section and the pdf is not showing on preview with default zoom and showing smaller.

read the skills and fix this simple bug

this is the real problem.
</user_query>

The PDF preview is rendering too small — I'll read the project skills and trace how the apply page iframe is sized versus the company workspace.

[REDACTED]

PDF iframes are likely sized while their panels are hidden on load. I'll fix iframe height to match the company page and refresh previews when switching tabs.

[REDACTED]

Fixed. Two things were causing the tiny PDF preview on `/apply`:

**Root cause:** PDF iframes were loading while the Master resumes / Projects panels were still hidden (Profile is the default tab). The browser PDF viewer sized itself to a zero-width hidden container, so the page rendered zoomed-out and small.

**Changes:**

1. **`apply.js`** — When you switch to Master resumes or Projects, the PDF preview reloads with a fresh `src` so the iframe gets the real viewport dimensions.

2. **`styles.css`** — PDF iframe height is now a fixed `520px` (same as the company workspace), instead of `height: 100%` which collapsed when the parent had no layout. LaTeX editor min-height bumped to `520px` to match. The earlier `flex: 1` on the editor/preview grid is kept so the layout still stretches.

Hard-refresh `/apply` (`styles.css?v=57`, `apply.js?v=11`) and switch to Master resumes or Projects — the PDF should render at normal size on both tabs.

[REDACTED]
